Hi, I'm Fran Diaz. I help homeowners understand the condition of their roof and guide them through the process — from storm damage evaluations to full replacements — with clear communication and zero stress.

Most of the homeowners I work with come through referrals and repeat clients, which is something I take a lot of pride in.

Not sure if your roof has damage? I'm always happy to take a look and provide an honest assessment.
🔍 Schedule a Free Roof Inspection 📞 (970) 689-5593 💬 Text Fran See My Reviews ↓
What I Offer
How I can help you
🔍
Storm Damage Evaluation
A thorough roof inspection where I carefully check for hail, wind, or storm damage and document everything with detailed photos.
You receive an honest, easy-to-understand evaluation so you know exactly where things stand and what your options are.
For homeowners after storms
📋
Insurance Claim Guidance
Helping homeowners understand and navigate the insurance process when legitimate storm damage is present — step by step.
Clear guidance so nothing gets overlooked, everything is documented, and the experience is far less stressful.
Unsure about the process?
🏠
Roof Replacement & Repairs
High-quality roof replacements and repairs using proven materials and proper installation practices.
A properly installed roofing system to protect your home long-term — with clear communication from start to finish.
Damaged or aging roof?
My Approach
The way I work

Most of my work comes from referrals and repeat clients — and that's no accident. I place a strong emphasis on doing things right, every single time.

Real Work. Real Results.
Project showcase

Browse by project type to see exactly the kind of work you need done.

F01
Hail Damage Replacement · Colorado
IKO Nordic Class 4 Impact-Resistant · Granite Black
Before Hail Damage Throughout · Significant Granule Loss
Significant Hail Damage
Those black spots across the entire roof are hail impact marks — each one knocked off granules and exposed the mat underneath, leaving the home vulnerable to leaks.
Damage Across Every Plane
No section of the roof was spared. Widespread impact marks across multiple planes confirmed a full replacement was the only responsible solution.
↓
During Installation
↓
During Full Tear-Off · Ice & Water Shield · Materials Staged
Tear-Off In Progress
Old shingles and underlayment stripped down to the deck — full tear-off underway before new materials are installed.
Ice & Water Shield + Underlayment
Ice and water shield installed along the eaves followed by Bulldog Roofing underlayment across the full deck — two layers of protection before a single shingle goes down.
Materials Staged & Ready
IKO Nordic shingles staged along the deck with Bulldog underlayment already down — crew finishing the tear-off while the next stage is prepped and ready to go.
↓
The Finished Result
↓
After IKO Nordic Class 4 Impact-Resistant · Granite Black
Ridge Detail
Tight, uniform installation along the ridge — IKO Nordic Class 4 shingles in Granite Black, rated to resist future hail impacts.
Ridge Cap & Ventilation
New ridge cap installed with proper ventilation — Class 4 impact-resistant shingles also qualify many homeowners for insurance premium discounts.
Job Complete
The full picture — a premium IKO Nordic Granite Black roof protecting this Colorado home and built to take on whatever storm comes next.
F02
Full Roof Replacement + Flat Roof · Denver, CO
IKO Nordic Class 4 · New Flat Roof Membrane · Rotted Deck Replacement
Before Failing Mod Bit + Hail Damaged Shingles
Failing Mod Bit Flat Roof
Old modified bitumen flat roof with cracked, dried-out seams — water had been actively leaking into the home for some time before we were called out.
Hail Damage — Pitched Roof
The pitched section had its own problems — chalk-marked hail impacts and heavy granule loss across the entire field. Both systems needed to be replaced.
↓
During Installation
↓
During Rotted Deck Removed · Fresh OSB · Ice & Water Shield
Rotted Deck Discovered
Tear-off revealed severely rotted decking — years of water intrusion from the failing mod bit had destroyed the structure underneath. All rotted wood removed and replaced before anything else proceeded.
Fresh Deck + Ice & Water Shield
New OSB installed over both sections with ice and water shield rolled out across the deck — a proper foundation before a single shingle or membrane goes down.
↓
The Finished Result
↓
After IKO Nordic Class 4 + New Flat Roof Membrane · Denver, CO
Pitched Roof Complete
New IKO Nordic Class 4 shingles installed across the entire pitched roof — clean, uniform, and built to handle Colorado's hail season.
Both Systems Complete
The full picture — new IKO Nordic Class 4 shingles on the pitched section and a brand new flat roof membrane where the old mod bit had been failing. Two systems, one job, done right.
New Chimney Flashing
New black chimney flashing sealed tight around the base — a critical detail that's often overlooked but is one of the most common sources of roof leaks.
